item.title: US Premarket Movers for July 2, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures were little changed as of 7:53 a.m. New York time as investors rotate out of the high-flying momentum stocks that powered last quarter’s rally.

item.title: Leveraged ETFs Are Just the Tip of the Iceberg
item.description: Record leverage is sweeping through the financial system, exposing hedge funds, banks, retail traders and money market funds to potential mounting losses.

item.title: Bad News for LNG Buyers as Qatar’s Export Revival Falters
item.description: While oil is flowing again through Hormuz, fewer gas tankers are making the still-risky crossing.

item.title: US Payrolls Growth Expected to Be Solid
item.description: Jobseekers during a job fair in Buffalo, New York. Photographer: Lauren Petracca/Bloomberg

item.title: JPMorgan Joins Gulf Banks on $7 Billion Loan for Syrian Projects
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. is joining Gulf lenders to help arrange about $7 billion of debt financing for a Qatari firm’s projects in Syria, marking one of the biggest foreign commitments to the country’s reconstruction since dictator Bashar al-Assad was ousted by rebel forces.

item.title: Fed’s Kevin Warsh Lays Out His Optimism Over AI in Global Stage Debut
item.description: The US central bank chief avoids forward guidance, outlines bullish AI productivity case.

item.title: Saudi Supertankers Exit Hormuz in Kingdom’s Biggest Oil Flow Since Iran War Truce
item.description: Saudi Arabia is exporting the most crude from inside the Persian Gulf since the Iran war blocked the Strait of Hormuz, as producers across the region boost shipments following an interim peace deal between Washington and Tehran.

item.title: EU Struggles to Find Consensus to Confront China on Trade
item.description: Leaders across Europe can’t agree on how tough to get with Beijing on trade

item.title: Meta Looks to Make Money From Its AI Spending Binge
item.description: The company is considering a cloud computing business to help generate revenue from AI

item.title: Skyroot Plans India’s First Private Sector Rocket Launch in July
item.description: Skyroot Aerospace Pvt announced that it was gearing up for its maiden flight of Vikram-1 — India’s first privately designed and developed orbital rocket — between July 12 and August 4, in what would be a critical milestone for the country’s first space unicorn after facing several delays.

item.title: Finance and Tech Cut 28,000 Jobs Monthly as AI Takes Hold
item.description: Signs of slower hiring, attrition and companies deciding not to replace exiting workers.

item.title: Investors Are Using Leveraged ETFs to Turbocharge AI Bets
item.description: Funds have begun to magnify swings in stocks like SK Hynix, analysts say

item.title: Russian Gasoline Shortage Begins to Spill Into Central Asia
item.description: Russia’s gasoline-supply crunch is starting to ripple into Central Asia, where some countries lack sufficient domestic fuel production and have long relied on Russian imports.

item.title: French Heat Wave Kills Poultry, Corn Crops in Key Farming Nation
item.description: France’s record-breaking heat has cut as much as 30% of corn production in Europe’s top farming nation and killed hundreds of thousands of poultry.

item.title: UK Consumer Loan Default Gauge Jumps to Highest Since 2009
item.description: A gauge measuring Britons defaulting on credit cards and other unsecured loans has hit its highest level since the financial crisis, a sign of growing pressure on household finances.

item.title: Warren Urges Fed’s Waller To Halt Work On Reserve Bank Proposal
item.description: Senator Elizabeth Warren is asking Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller to halt his efforts to overhaul operations at the central bank’s 12 regional branches.

item.title: Treasuries Slip Before Payrolls as Traders Weigh Warsh Comments
item.description: Treasuries edged lower as traders awaited a US employment print that may give clues on the Federal Reserve’s interest-rate path after Chairman Kevin Warsh struck a dovish tone on inflation.

item.title: China and US Target Tariff Reductions on Agricultural Products
item.description: China and the US are seeking to roll back tariffs on some agricultural products, as they look to preserve a broader trade truce that was struck last year.

item.title: Brazil Rail Operator Opens New Route to Boost Soymeal Exports
item.description: Brazilian railway operator VLI SA is starting a new export route for soybean meal, addressing key bottlenecks as the country attempts to boost shipments of the animal feed ingredient.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Among Banks Challenging Growing Dollar Optimism
item.description: Currency strategists at Credit Agricole, Morgan Stanley and TD Securities are among those bucking the consensus call for a stronger dollar.

item.title: SpaceX Analyst Debut Set to Test $2.2 Trillion Valuation
item.description: Investors in SpaceX have been largely flying blind since the company’s record-breaking IPO last month, with few financial projections to help determine what the stock is actually worth.

item.title: SBI Funds’ $1.2 Billion India IPO Launch in July to Test Demand
item.description: SBI Funds Management Ltd. is set to launch its initial public offering, which could raise up to $1.2 billion, in the week starting July 13, according to people familiar with the matter, testing investor demand ahead of a busy pipeline of billion-dollar listings in India.

item.title: What Dan Wang Saw on His Last Trip to China
item.description: China's great economic contradiction.

item.title: What Dan Wang Saw on His Last Trip to China
item.description: What Dan Wang Saw on His Last Trip to China【原文：What Dan Wang Saw on His Last Trip to China】

item.title: Japan Retail Investors Bought Record Stocks Last Week in Selloff
item.description: Japan’s retail investors purchased a record amount of local shares last week, when a tech selloff sent equity gauges plunging.

item.title: SAP Restricts Hiring, Travel to Fund ‘Significant’ AI Push
item.description: SAP SE said it will cut back hiring and travel to save costs as Europe’s largest software company devotes more resources to developing artificial intelligence technologies and fending off new competitors.

item.title: Google Loses EU Top Court Fight Over €4.1 Billion Android Fine
item.description: Google lost its long-running fight against a €4.1 billion ($4.7 billion) European Union antitrust fine after the bloc’s top judges said regulators were right to punish the US giant for abusing Android’s market power.

item.title: SoftBank Plans AI Cloud Services in US to Tap Surging Demand
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp. and its telecom unit will start renting AI computing resources to US companies next fiscal year in a bid to capitalize on the company’s growing pipeline of data center projects.

item.title: Quantum Systems More Than Doubles Valuation to $8 Billion
item.description: Quantum Systems has raised $1.2 billion in a funding round, more than doubling the German drone startup’s valuation to about $8 billion post-money, in the latest example of investors’ enthusiasm for defense-related bets.

item.title: Japan’s Tax Revenue Rose to Another Record in Latest Fiscal Year
item.description: Japan’s national tax revenue climbed to a record in the fiscal year ended in March as wage hikes, rising employment and earnings on financial assets boosted income tax receipts.
